caption: Slice 1 of the nested atomic order queue (#826): the reform-before-move hold (a plain move's pause, and a form-up's reshape hold shown here) is now driven by a REFORM leaf order's own `reform_timer`/`reform_until_settled`/`reform_settle_eps` fields instead of the old bare `Unit._reform_timer`/`_reform_target`/`_reform_until_settled`/`_reform_settle_eps` fields. The maneuver ITSELF is unchanged -- watch the lone Spearmen regiment hold in place for about 5 seconds while it reshapes onto the wider drag-line frontage (state-dump verified: `order_phase` reads REFORM from tick 40 through tick ~335), then release into its march once the ranks settle, arriving at the new line right as this clip ends (`order_phase` reads MARCH from tick ~340, and the regiment reaches its destination by tick ~1400 -- all inside this clip's extended runtime, before combat starts around tick 1450). One real, observable side effect: an ORDINARY move/form-up's reform-before-move pause (the common case, not just this composite/reshape one) used to hold on bare fields with `order.phase` staying NONE, so the HUD read "Holding position" and a state dump's `order_phase` read "NONE" throughout. It now holds on a REFORM leaf like every other reform hold, so it reads "Re-forming"/`REFORM` instead -- a more accurate label, not a functional change, but worth knowing if you're diffing HUD text or transcripts against an older build. The rear-move composite's own interstitial reform (the same mechanism, now shared instead of duplicated) is covered by the GUT suite but isn't shown in this clip.
